title: TaskFlow desktop app crashes immediately on Save (macOS 14.2, v2.3.x)
problem: The TaskFlow desktop app terminates abruptly every time the user clicks Save on their task list. A brief error dialog appears to flash on screen before the app closes, but it disappears too quickly to read. The user loses unsaved work each time.
root_cause_hypothesis: Most likely an unhandled exception during the save/serialization path — possibly a data-specific issue triggered by the contents of this user's task list (special characters, size, or a corrupt entry). The fleeting error dialog suggests the app does catch the error but the crash handler or dialog is immediately followed by process exit.
reproduction_steps: Open TaskFlow desktop app v2.3.x on macOS 14.2
Create or load a task list with multiple tasks
Observe: app window disappears immediately; a brief error flash may be visible
environment: macOS 14.2 (Sonoma), TaskFlow desktop app, version approximately 2.3.1
impact: User is completely unable to save any work. Data loss occurs on every save attempt. This is a blocking workflow issue — the user has been affected for multiple days.
recommended_fix: 1. Check macOS crash reports (~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports) for TaskFlow crash logs to identify the exact exception. 2. Review the save/serialization code path for unhandled exceptions, particularly around data encoding, file I/O permissions, and large list handling. 3. Investigate the crash dialog lifecycle — it should remain visible or log its contents rather than flashing and closing. 4. Attempt to reproduce with varying task list sizes and content to isolate whether the crash is data-dependent.
proposed_test_case: Create a task list with various content (empty, small, large, special characters, unicode) and invoke the save operation on macOS 14.x. Verify that save completes without crash and that the file is written correctly. Additionally, test that if save does fail, the error dialog remains visible and the app does not terminate.
information_gaps: Crash log or diagnostic report contents from macOS
Number and content of tasks in the list (could indicate a size or data-specific trigger)
Whether this started after an app update or a specific change to the task list
